content:
This teaching is going to specifically deal with how to find explosive price moves before they happen.
Points of focus in this module:
- Commitment of Traders (COT)
- Weekly Range Contraction
- Daily Range Contraction

section_title: UNDERSTANDING THE COMMITMENT OF TRADERS (COT) REPORT
content:
We're taking a look at a Commitment of Traders report in the form of a line graph or net Traders position
chart. This is a longstanding staple in technical analysis. Larry Williams popularized this from his work with
the Commitment of Traders or CFTC reportable report.
I am only interested in tracking the red line, which represents Commercial Speculators. By following this on
a longer-term time frame, it will help give warning signs when there's a potential impending reversal, which
when it's a reversal, they sometimes can be explosive in nature.

section_title: MONITORING COMMERCIAL EXTREMES FOR SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
The CFTC weekly net Traders position is the data that creates this graph. What we do is we monitor 12
- month commercial extremes.
This means:
- The highest they've been in the last 12 months
- Or the lowest they've been in the last 12 months
We look for the levels where they reflect net extremes; in other words, they will qualify support and
resistance on a higher time frame chart.

section_title: THE ZERO LINE AND TRADER BEHAVIOR
content:
First, we have to draw in our zero line here. The zero line delineates anything above the zero line as buying
and below it as selling.
- The blue speculators (Street money) usually have no idea what's going on.
- The large speculators (green line) are going to be diametrically opposed to the commercials. This does
not mean that they're wrong; it just means that they are the ones that are Trend following in nature.

As the market dropped lower on the Euro, the green line depicts that the large speculators were selling
short (below the zero line denotes selling). Above it denotes buying.
We can see the commercials were buying all through this decline. While you might wonder how this is
useful, we want to look at trend and also at key levels that the commercials support.

At this low here, the commercials had the largest position of longs at the time, and the market created a
low, went sideways, and then dropped down one more time. We had an increase of buying by the
commercials.
We add our first area of buying interest by the commercials and the second one here. They're ramping up,
with a big jump up in the red line, and another one over here where they're above the zero line and we're at
that same level where it's bounced before. This could be a key support level.

section_title: COMMERCIAL INSIGHT INTO SUPPORT AND RESISTANCE
content:
Notice that the commercials are giving you insight that you otherwise wouldn't glean if you look at just
simple classic support and resistance theories. It takes you back to the question: 'which support, which
resistance?'
From a higher time frame standpoint, this is one of the things I like to teach because it gives you a context
behind support and resistance levels that in my opinion are key.

section_title: RECENT PRICE ACTION AND COMMERCIAL SHIFT
content:
With this understanding, we know that the commercials have been bullish for a long period of time, like two
years, buying, buying, buying, building up large positions. Then we had a rally up.
Notice that this reaction off here was below these two equal lows. Not surprisingly, this move up has taken
us above these two relatively equal highs, and we are potentially in an area where we could come back
down a little bit lower.

section_title: COMMERCIAL BEARISH HEDGING AND FUTURE PRICE MOVES
content:
This takes me to the area of business on the commercials now because look where they're at: we're below
the zero line. So they may be indicating a longer-term shift in the direction on the Euro. We could see it
come down a little bit lower.
This is a warning flag, if you will, to monitor what the Euro is most likely going to do, at least from the long
- term standpoint. The Commercial Traders, folks that are long-term hedgers, they're already hedging in
some bearishness on the Eurodollar. I use this information to kind of build the idea around potential future
explosive price moves.

- POWER OF THREE CONCEPT
Next, we're going to talk about weekly range contraction. As you can see, when we use the Open-High
- Low-Close bar (Power of Three), it's a graphic depiction.
- If we're bullish, we're going to be looking at the open price and then a drop down, buying anywhere
between the opening price or just a little bit above (still okay), but mainly looking for a move below the
opening price.
- When we're bearish, we're looking for the opening price and then a move up to sell or fade that rally and
then ride it lower for a down close.
This assumes we know there's going to be a large range day or a directional bias that lends well to this
technique. Power of Three capitalizes on range expansion, so we need to know the likelihood of this
phenomenon happening, otherwise we're going to do it wrong.

If the opposite of range expansion is contraction, how can we find that? The Candlestick chart helps
highlight this.
If we look at a candlestick like this, say this is a typical large range day, which would look like something
like this open-high-low-close here. It could be very close to that in terms of the body portion between the
open and the close. So we have the open down here and the close up here if this is a bullish candle.

section_title: SMALLER BODIES PRECEDE LARGER EXPLOSIVE MOVES
content:
If this was the body of, say for instance, a Tuesday, and then say Wednesday we had this type of body, we
have a smaller bodied candle. This means that we could see a large range day on Thursday or Friday. It's
more or less a precursor, if you will, because smaller bodies precede larger bodies.
If we're always looking for action in the marketplace and it's already moving, you're chasing price. So you
want to anticipate when price is going to have an explosive or large range or big move or series of big
moves. It's going to happen by studying the bodies. If the bodies are getting smaller, it's going to be a
directional move in magnitude that's larger than it has been in recent days or weeks.

section_title: FOCUSING ON CANDLE BODIES FOR RANGE CONTRACTION
content:
Again, depicting the top of the body on here and contrasting it with this one here, so that's the difference of
what I'm looking for.
For weekly range contraction, we're going to be looking for candles that begin to compress, ranges that
have smaller bodies. That's the key, those are the ones that we're looking for, and we're wanting to trade
when they form. Big ranges come after small ranges form, and we ignore the wicks; we're focusing only on
the bodies of the candles.

- SWING LOW EXAMPLE
Let's take a couple examples here. As you can see, at this swing low, we've had a large body candle, large
body candle, and then look how small the body gets here. Then we have a turning point. Price has been
moving up, up, we went sideways, and now look what happens: the bodies between the open and the
close, they get very, very small.
Disregard the wicks; try to block that out. Focus only on the open and close relationship. When that gets
small, what's about to happen is you're going to see an expansion.

section_title: APPLYING POWER OF THREE WITH WEEKLY CONTRACTION
content:
This is when anticipating Power of Three can be applied:
- The open here, small down move, buying, holding for the close near the end of the day at the top of the
range.
Similar scenario here, which we can see again. Small bodies form after that large one. We know there's
going to be a large range coming, so we want to be focusing on the trend. It's been bullish, so we can be
looking for:
- Open, decline, buy it, look for expansion on the upside.
- Next same scenario: open, down movement, buy it, hold for the close.

- SWING HIGH EXAMPLE
Similarly, we can see at this swing high, we have a small little body in here. Again, disregard the wicks.
Again, a small bodied candle in here, disregarding the wicks, and large moves after each one of these.
We're seeing large, this is the weekly chart, so there's a lot of pips in there.
Another opportunity here, you can barely even see a body in this, very, very indicative of a large range
move, and we see a series of three weeks that come by way of bullishness, over 300 and some pips
available from that one.

section_title: RECENT WEEKLY RANGE CONTRACTION AND ANTICIPATION
content:
Then we have a small range again. So what's going to happen? We're looking for range expansion. The
candle forms, and now at the time of this recording, we have a very small body candle again.
So weekly range has been compressing. We're looking for a large weekly range next.

- SAME PRINCIPLES, DAILY VIEW
Now, obviously, as I said with the weekly range, everything is the same for a daily range contraction. So
without repeating myself, I have it here for your notes. But let's take a look at a couple examples from the
daily perspective.
We can see up in here after the swing high, we have a very small body candle. Disregard the wick; we don't
care about that. We're looking at the body of the candle, very small little compression. If we are seeing the
range compression and contract smaller and tighter, we know there's going to be a large expansion. So the
next move, if it's directionally determined higher or lower, we can use the Power of Three concept in that
light.

- BEARISH EXAMPLE
Here we see it's bearish:
- Open, rally, sell the high, hold for the close.
So we have several days of down movement. Look at the large ranges that just follow right after that small
range body.

- INSIDE DAY INTERPRETATION
Next opportunity comes in here. Price trades down at an old low. Price has a big range down, and then we
have a small range day here. So it's an inside day relative to the body.
This is my insight on inside days; I don't care about the wicks. Most trading circles, teaching, authors,
gurus, whatever, they would never classify this as an inside day because we have this long wicked portion
of the candle that went below the previous day. That would negate this being called an inside day. But from
the standpoint that I'm teaching range expansion and contraction, this is in fact an inside day.

- RULE OF THUMB FOR LOOKBACK
That means there's going to be a large range expansion or a series of movement that's directional after this
day forms. We see that here, several up close days, and it creates a swing high. Range gets small here, so
we know that there's going to be another series of movement. We have down closed candles right in here.
We have small ranges relative to what's happened recently.
There's a rule of thumb I use: I want to see what's happened. If it's a daily chart, I want to see what's
happened in the last 5 to 7 days. I look back about 5 to 7 days for range contraction and expansion. So that
filter for volatility on the daily chart, you want to look at about 5 days, which is a typical trading week, but
then I also use 7 days because it may have to look back into the previous week as well.

- RELATIVE SMALLNESS AND EXPANSION
That gives me this whole criterion here, and we can see that there's a large range day in the last seven
days here, so that means these are relatively small in comparison. So we know there's going to be an
expansion due. Large range day, large range day, and then we go smaller.
Looking back, we have a large range day here, so these are relatively small. So we know what's coming:
large range days, big range day.

- LOW REJECTION AND EXPANSION
Down here at an old low, we trade below that low, and now look what happens: we have small ranges,
barely any range here at all. Big expansion on the upside, and then we go into a small range here. Big
ranges come.

- SWING HIGH REJECTION
Right in here after a swing high, small little body. Again, disregard the wicks; they'll trip you up. That's all
stop running on both sides of the marketplace. They're taking all the Traders out and going right back into
the middle, and then the big move takes place down, explosive price action on the sell side, reaching for an
old low rejection.

And now at the time of this recording, last Friday, we have a small range body again. So we know there's
going to be expansion due coming in the next couple days, so we would look for large range days to form.
As a Trader, we want to see movement, and by having a study of when that movement is expected, not just
where to buy, where to sell, you have to have an expectation for magnitude and velocity. How much can the
move be seen before it happens? Is there going to be excitement, and is it going to be animated, if you will?
What I do is I look for when things are getting quiet. That means the Traders are going to get bored with it,
and when they're not paying attention to it, it takes off, and then everybody else in the retail world will
chase it once it's expanding. I want to be in before the big move happens because I'm most likely going to
be in while it's expanding up long or expanding lower and being bearish.
